category: 56: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services / 5612: Facilities Support Services
description: This dataset represents the operation of 4-str forklifts using gasoline as a fuel source. Emissions and fuel consumption data are calculated using EPA's Motor Vehicle Emission Simulator (MOVES), the regulatory model to determine emission inventories for equipements used in construction, arguculture, industry and commercial sectors.  The reference flow is the opertation of equipment for 1 MJ of energy provided.

flow.category: Technosphere Flows / 56: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services / 5612: Facilities Support Services
flow.name: Operation of equipment; 4-stroke forklifts

flow.category: Technosphere flows/44-45: Retail Trade/4471: Gasoline Stations
flow.name: Gasoline; dispensed at pump

name: Operation of equipment; 4-stroke forklifts; gasoline powered

processDocumentation.completenessDescription: The results include emissions from running exhaust, crankcase venting, refueling vapor and spillage,  evaporative tank permeation, evaporative hose permeation, and fuel vapor venting from diurnal, hot soak, and running activity. Evaporative emissions while the vehicle is soaking (engine off) are not included. Manufacturing of vehicles, roads, and other infrastructure are not included in this dataset. The model for nonroad equipments does not include nitrous oxide (N2O).
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: MOVES runs were performed using MOVES4.0.1 in 2024.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: MOVES runs were performed at the default settings at the state level.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription: Emissions are calculated from an adjusted emission factor multiplied by rated power, load factor, engine population, and activity. The model uses base-year equipment populations by technology type and model year, and applies growth factors to estimate the population in the analysis year. Nonroad emissions by equipment type, fuel type, and age are estimated based on median life at full load, load factors, activity, and age distributions. Equipment populations are allocated to the county level using surrogate data.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: This process represents emissions from equipment operated in all U.S. states.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed by the Federal LCA Commons Data Curator Eastern Research Group, Inc. (ERG) under subcontract (SUB-2024-10066) to the National Renewable Energy Laboratory under Department of Energy (DOE) Prime Contract No. DE-AC36-08GO28308. The work is funded by DOE's Advanced Materials & Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO).

processDocumentation.samplingDescription: Emissions and activity data for each state are combined to generate an activity-weighted average for each region. No uncertainty was assessed.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription: 4-Str Forklifts reflects the gasoline powered 4-str forklifts used in the industrial sector with an average horsepower of 99 hp and a load factor of 0.3.
processDocumentation.timeDescription: This process reflects the equipments as estimated in 2020. MOVES estimates daily emissions for weekdays and  weekends by month and year for calendar years 1990 and 1999 through 2060.
processDocumentation.useAdvice: This process is intended to connect to fuel supply processes from the Heavy Equipment repository.
